verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचअथ कृष्ण: परिवृतो ज्ञातिभिर्मुदितात्मभि: ।अनुगीयमानो न्यविशद्‌‌व्रजं गोकुलमण्डितम् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaatha kṛṣṇaḥ parivṛtojñātibhir muditātmabhiḥanugīyamāno nyaviśadvrajaṁ gokula-maṇḍitam
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Surrounded by His blissful companions, who constantly chanted His glories, Śrī Kṛṣṇa then entered the village of Vraja, which was decorated with herds of cows.

verses.sanskrit: व्रजे विक्रीडतोरेवं गोपालच्छद्ममायया ।ग्रीष्मो नामर्तुरभवन्नातिप्रेयाञ्छरीरिणाम् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: vraje vikrīḍator evaṁgopāla-cchadma-māyayāgrīṣmo nāmartur abhavannāti-preyāñ charīriṇām
verses.text: While Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma were thus enjoying life in Vṛndāvana in the guise of ordinary cowherd boys, the summer season gradually appeared. This season is not very pleasing to embodied souls.

verses.sanskrit: स च वृन्दावनगुणैर्वसन्त इव लक्षित: ।यत्रास्ते भगवान् साक्षाद् रामेण सह केशव: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa ca vṛndāvana-guṇairvasanta iva lakṣitaḥyatrāste bhagavān sākṣādrāmeṇa saha keśavaḥ
verses.text: Nevertheless, because the Supreme Personality of Godhead was personally staying in Vṛndāvana along with Balarāma, summer manifested the qualities of spring. Such are the features of the land of Vṛndāvana.

verses.sanskrit: यत्र निर्झरनिर्ह्रादनिवृत्तस्वनझिल्लिकम् ।शश्वत्तच्छीकरर्जीषद्रुममण्डलमण्डितम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yatra nirjhara-nirhrāda-nivṛtta-svana-jhillikamśaśvat tac-chīkararjīṣa-druma-maṇḍala-maṇḍitam
verses.text: In Vṛndāvana, the loud sound of waterfalls covered the crickets’ noise, and clusters of trees constantly moistened by spray from those waterfalls beautified the entire area.

verses.sanskrit: सरित्सर:प्रस्रवणोर्मिवायुनाकह्लारकुञ्जोत्पलरेणुहारिणा ।न विद्यते यत्र वनौकसां दवोनिदाघवह्न्यर्कभवोऽतिशाद्वले ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarit-saraḥ-prasravaṇormi-vāyunākahlāra-kañjotpala-reṇu-hāriṇāna vidyate yatra vanaukasāṁ davonidāgha-vahny-arka-bhavo ’ti-śādvale
verses.text: The wind wafting over the waves of the lakes and flowing rivers carried away the pollen of many varieties of lotuses and water lilies and then cooled the entire Vṛndāvana area. Thus the residents there did not suffer from the heat generated by the blazing summer sun and seasonal forest fires. Indeed, Vṛndāvana was abundant with fresh green grass.

verses.sanskrit: अगाधतोयह्रदिनीतटोर्मिभि-र्द्रवत्पुरीष्या: पुलिनै: समन्तत: ।न यत्र चण्डांशुकरा विषोल्बणाभुवो रसं शाद्वलितं च गृह्णते ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: agādha-toya-hradinī-taṭormibhirdravat-purīṣyāḥ pulinaiḥ samantataḥna yatra caṇḍāṁśu-karā viṣolbaṇābhuvo rasaṁ śādvalitaṁ ca gṛhṇate
verses.text: With their flowing waves the deep rivers drenched their banks, making them damp and muddy. Thus the rays of the sun, which were as fierce as poison, could not evaporate the earth’s sap or parch its green grass.

verses.sanskrit: वनं कुसुमितं श्रीमन्नदच्चित्रमृगद्विजम् ।गायन्मयूरभ्रमरं कूजत्कोकिलसारसम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: vanaṁ kusumitaṁ śrīmannadac-citra-mṛga-dvijamgāyan mayūra-bhramaraṁkūjat-kokila-sārasam
verses.text: Flowers beautifully decorated the forest of Vṛndāvana, and many varieties of animals and birds filled it with sound. The peacocks and bees sang, and the cuckoos and cranes cooed.

verses.sanskrit: क्रीडिष्यमाणस्तन् कृष्णो भगवान् बलसंयुत: ।वेणुं विरणयन् गोपैर्गोधनै: संवृतोऽविशत् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: krīḍiṣyamāṇas tat krṣṇobhagavān bala-saṁyutaḥveṇuṁ viraṇayan gopairgo-dhanaiḥ saṁvṛto ’viśat
verses.text: Intending to engage in pastimes, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, accompanied by Lord Balarāma and surrounded by the cowherd boys and the cows, entered the forest of Vṛndāvana as He played His flute.

verses.sanskrit: प्रवालबर्हस्तबकस्रग्धातुकृतभूषणा: ।रामकृष्णादयो गोपा ननृतुर्युयुधुर्जगु: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: pravāla-barha-stabaka-srag-dhātu-kṛta-bhūṣaṇāḥrāma-kṛṣṇādayo gopānanṛtur yuyudhur jaguḥ
verses.text: Decorating themselves with newly grown leaves, along with peacock feathers, garlands, clusters of flower buds, and colored minerals, Balarāma, Kṛṣṇa and Their cowherd friends danced, wrestled and sang.

verses.sanskrit: कृष्णस्य नृत्यत: केचिज्जगु: केचिदवादयन् ।वेणुपाणितलै: श‍ृङ्गै: प्रशशंसुरथापरे ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛṣṇasya nṛtyataḥ kecijjaguḥ kecid avādayanveṇu-pāṇitalaiḥ śṛṅgaiḥpraśaśaṁsur athāpare
verses.text: As Kṛṣṇa danced, some of the boys accompanied Him by singing, and others by playing flutes, hand cymbals and buffalo horns, while still others praised His dancing.

verses.sanskrit: गोपजातिप्रतिच्छन्ना देवा गोपालरूपिणौ ।ईडिरे कृष्णरामौ च नटा इव नटं नृप ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: gopa-jāti-praticchannādevā gopāla-rūpiṇauīḍire kṛṣṇa-rāmau canaṭā iva naṭaṁ nṛpa
verses.text: O King, demigods disguised themselves as members of the cowherd community and, just as dramatic dancers praise another dancer, worshiped Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, who were also appearing as cowherd boys.

verses.sanskrit: भ्रमणैर्लङ्घनै: क्षेपैरास्फोटनविकर्षणै: ।चिक्रीडतुर्नियुद्धेन काकपक्षधरौ क्‍वचित् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhramaṇair laṅghanaiḥ kṣepairāsphoṭana-vikarṣaṇaiḥcikrīḍatur niyuddhenakāka-pakṣa-dharau kvacit
verses.text: Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma played with their cowherd boyfriends by whirling about, leaping, hurling, slapping and fighting. Sometimes Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma would pull the hair on the boys’ heads.

verses.sanskrit: क्‍वचिन्नृत्यत्सु चान्येषु गायकौ वादकौ स्वयम् ।शशंसतुर्महाराज साधु साध्विति वादिनौ ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kvacin nṛtyatsu cānyeṣugāyakau vādakau svayamśaśaṁsatur mahā-rājasādhu sādhv iti vādinau
verses.text: While the other boys were dancing, O King, Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma would sometimes accompany them with song and instrumental music, and sometimes the two Lords would praise the boys, saying, “Very good! Very good!”

verses.sanskrit: क्‍वचिद्ब‍िल्वै: क्‍वचित्कुम्भै: क्‍वचामलकमुष्टिभि: ।अस्पृश्यनेत्रबन्धाद्यै: क्‍वचिन्मृगखगेहया ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kvacid bilvaiḥ kvacit kumbhaiḥkvacāmalaka-muṣṭibhiḥaspṛśya-netra-bandhādyaiḥkvacin mṛga-khagehayā
verses.text: Sometimes the cowherd boys would play with bilva or kumbha fruits, and sometimes with handfuls of āmalaka fruits. At other times they would play the games of trying to touch one another or of trying to identify somebody while one is blindfolded, and sometimes they would imitate animals and birds.

verses.sanskrit: क्‍वचिच्च दर्दुरप्लावैर्विविधैरुपहासकै: ।कदाचित् स्यन्दोलिकया कर्हिचिन्नृपचेष्टया ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kvacic ca dardura-plāvairvividhair upahāsakaiḥkadācit syandolikayākarhicin nṛpa-ceṣṭayā
verses.text: They would sometimes jump around like frogs, sometimes play various jokes, sometimes ride in swings and sometimes imitate monarchs.

verses.sanskrit: एवं तौ लोकसिद्धाभि: क्रीडाभिश्चेरतुर्वने ।नद्यद्रिद्रोणिकुञ्जेषु काननेषु सर:सु च ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ tau loka-siddhābhiḥkrīḍābhiś ceratur vanenady-adri-droṇi-kuñjeṣukānaneṣu saraḥsu ca
verses.text: In this way Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma played all sorts of well-known games as They wandered among the rivers, hills, valleys, bushes, trees and lakes of Vṛndāvana.

verses.sanskrit: पशूंश्चारयतोर्गोपैस्तद्वने रामकृष्णयो: ।गोपरूपी प्रलम्बोऽगादसुरस्तज्जिहीर्षया ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: paśūṁś cārayator gopaistad-vane rāma-kṛṣṇayoḥgopa-rūpī pralambo ’gādasuras taj-jihīrṣayā
verses.text: While Rāma, Kṛṣṇa and Their cowherd friends were thus tending the cows in that Vṛndāvana forest, the demon Pralamba entered their midst. He had assumed the form of a cowherd boy with the intention of kidnapping Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma.

verses.sanskrit: तं विद्वानपि दाशार्हो भगवान् सर्वदर्शन: ।अन्वमोदत तत्सख्यं वधं तस्य विचिन्तयन् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ vidvān api dāśārhobhagavān sarva-darśanaḥanvamodata tat-sakhyaṁvadhaṁ tasya vicintayan
verses.text: Since the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa, who had appeared in the Daśārha dynasty, sees everything, He understood who the demon was. Still, the Lord pretended to accept the demon as a friend, while at the same time seriously considering how to kill him.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रोपाहूय गोपालान् कृष्ण: प्राह विहारवित् ।हे गोपा विहरिष्यामो द्वन्द्वीभूय यथायथम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatropāhūya gopālānkṛṣṇaḥ prāha vihāra-vithe gopā vihariṣyāmodvandvī-bhūya yathā-yatham
verses.text: Kṛṣṇa, who knows all sports and games, then called together the cowherd boys and spoke as follows: “Hey cowherd boys! Let’s play now! We’ll divide ourselves into two even teams.”

verses.sanskrit: तत्र चक्रु: परिवृढौ गोपा रामजनार्दनौ ।कृष्णसङ्घट्टिन: केचिदासन् रामस्य चापरे ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra cakruḥ parivṛḍhaugopā rāma-janārdanaukṛṣṇa-saṅghaṭṭinaḥ kecidāsan rāmasya cāpare
verses.text: The cowherd boys chose Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma as the leaders of the two parties. Some of the boys were on Kṛṣṇa’s side, and others joined Balarāma.

verses.sanskrit: आचेरुर्विविधा: क्रीडा वाह्यवाहकलक्षणा: ।यत्रारोहन्ति जेतारो वहन्ति च पराजिता: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ācerur vividhāḥ krīḍāvāhya-vāhaka-lakṣaṇāḥyatrārohanti jetārovahanti ca parājitāḥ
verses.text: The boys played various games involving carriers and passengers. In these games the winners would climb up on the backs of the losers, who would have to carry them.

verses.sanskrit: वहन्तो वाह्यमानाश्च चारयन्तश्च गोधनम् ।भाण्डीरकं नाम वटं जग्मु: कृष्णपुरोगमा: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: vahanto vāhyamānāś cacārayantaś ca go-dhanambhāṇḍīrakaṁ nāma vaṭaṁjagmuḥ kṛṣṇa-purogamāḥ
verses.text: Thus carrying and being carried by one another, and at the same time tending the cows, the boys followed Kṛṣṇa to a banyan tree known as Bhāṇḍīraka.

verses.sanskrit: रामसङ्घट्टिनो यर्हि श्रीदामवृषभादय: ।क्रीडायां जयिनस्तांस्तानूहु: कृष्णादयो नृप ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: rāma-saṅghaṭṭino yarhiśrīdāma-vṛṣabhādayaḥkrīḍāyāṁ jayinas tāṁs tānūhuḥ kṛṣṇādayo nṛpa
verses.text: My dear King Parīkṣit, when Śrīdāmā, Vṛṣabha and the other members of Lord Balarāma’s party were victorious in these games, Kṛṣṇa and His followers had to carry them.

verses.sanskrit: उवाह कृष्णो भगवान् श्रीदामानं पराजित: ।वृषभं भद्रसेनस्तु प्रलम्बो रोहिणीसुतम् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: uvāha kṛṣṇo bhagavānśrīdāmānaṁ parājitaḥvṛṣabhaṁ bhadrasenas tupralambo rohiṇī-sutam
verses.text: Defeated, the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa carried Śrīdāmā. Bhadrasena carried Vṛṣabha, and Pralamba carried Balarāma, the son of Rohiṇī.

verses.sanskrit: अविषह्यं मन्यमान: कृष्णं दानवपुङ्गव: ।वहन् द्रुततरं प्रागादवरोहणत: परम् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: aviṣahyaṁ manyamānaḥkṛṣṇaṁ dānava-puṅgavaḥvahan drutataraṁ prāgādavarohaṇataḥ param
verses.text: Considering Lord Kṛṣṇa invincible, that foremost demon [Pralamba] quickly carried Balarāma far beyond the spot where he was supposed to put his passenger down.

verses.sanskrit: तमुद्वहन् धरणिधरेन्द्रगौरवंमहासुरो विगतरयो निजं वपु: ।स आस्थित: पुरटपरिच्छदो बभौतडिद्‌‌द्युमानुडुपतिवाडिवाम्बुद: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam udvahan dharaṇi-dharendra-gauravaṁmahāsuro vigata-rayo nijaṁ vapuḥsa āsthitaḥ puraṭa-paricchado babhautaḍid-dyumān uḍupati-vāḍ ivāmbudaḥ
verses.text: As the great demon carried Balarāma, the Lord became as heavy as massive Mount Sumeru, and Pralamba had to slow down. He then resumed his actual form — an effulgent body that was covered with golden ornaments and that resembled a cloud flashing with lightning and carrying the moon.

verses.sanskrit: निरीक्ष्य तद्वपुरलमम्बरे चरत्प्रदीप्तद‍ृग् भ्रुकुटितटोग्रदंष्ट्रकम् ।ज्वलच्छिखं कटककिरीटकुण्डल-त्विषाद्भ‍ुतं हलधर ईषदत्रसत् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: nirīkṣya tad-vapur alam ambare caratpradīpta-dṛg bhru-kuṭi-taṭogra-daṁṣṭrakamjvalac-chikhaṁ kaṭaka-kirīṭa-kuṇḍala-tviṣādbhutaṁ haladhara īṣad atrasat
verses.text: When Lord Balarāma, who carries the plow weapon, saw the gigantic body of the demon as he moved swiftly in the sky — with his blazing eyes, fiery hair, terrible teeth reaching toward his scowling brows, and an amazing effulgence generated by his armlets, crown and earrings — the Lord seemed to become a little frightened.

verses.sanskrit: अथागतस्मृतिरभयो रिपुं बलोविहायसार्थमिव हरन्तमात्मन: ।रुषाहनच्छिरसि द‍ृढेन मुष्टिनासुराधिपो गिरिमिव वज्ररंहसा ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: athāgata-smṛtir abhayo ripuṁ balovihāya sārtham iva harantam ātmanaḥruṣāhanac chirasi dṛḍhena muṣṭināsurādhipo girim iva vajra-raṁhasā
verses.text: Remembering the actual situation, the fearless Balarāma understood that the demon was tṛying to kidnap Him and take Him away from His companions. The Lord then became furious and struck the demon’s head with His hard fist, just as Indra, the king of the demigods, strikes a mountain with his thunderbolt weapon.

verses.sanskrit: स आहत: सपदि विशीर्णमस्तकोमुखाद् वमन् रुधिरमपस्मृतोऽसुर: ।महारवं व्यसुरपतत् समीरयन्गिरिर्यथा मघवत आयुधाहत: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa āhataḥ sapadi viśīrṇa-mastakomukhād vaman rudhiram apasmṛto ’suraḥmahā-ravaṁ vyasur apatat samīrayangirir yathā maghavata āyudhāhataḥ
verses.text: Thus smashed by Balarāma’s fist, Pralamba’s head immediately cracked open. The demon vomited blood from his mouth and lost all consciousness, and then with a great noise he fell lifeless on the ground, like a mountain devastated by Indra.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्ट्वा प्रलम्बं निहतं बलेन बलशालिना ।गोपा: सुविस्मिता आसन्साधु साध्विति वादिन: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā pralambaṁ nihataṁbalena bala-śālināgopāḥ su-vismitā āsansādhu sādhv iti vādinaḥ
verses.text: The cowherd boys were most astonished to see how the powerful Balarāma had killed the demon Pralamba, and they exclaimed, “Excellent! Excellent!”

verses.sanskrit: आशिषोऽभिगृणन्तस्तं प्रशशंसुस्तदर्हणम् ।प्रेत्यागतमिवालिङ्‌‌ग्य प्रेमविह्वलचेतस: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: āśiṣo ’bhigṛṇantas taṁpraśaśaṁsus tad-arhaṇampretyāgatam ivāliṅgyaprema-vihvala-cetasaḥ
verses.text: They offered Balarāma profuse benedictions and then glorified Him, who deserves all glorification. Their minds overwhelmed with ecstatic love, they embraced Him as if He had come back from the dead.

verses.sanskrit: पापे प्रलम्बे निहते देवा: परमनिर्वृता: ।अभ्यवर्षन् बलं माल्यै: शशंसु: साधु साध्विति ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: pāpe pralambe nihatedevāḥ parama-nirvṛtāḥabhyavarṣan balaṁ mālyaiḥśaśaṁsuḥ sādhu sādhv iti
verses.text: The sinful Pralamba having been killed, the demigods felt extremely happy, and they showered flower garlands upon Lord Balarāma and praised the excellence of His deed.
